author: maximmenshikov_
content: <@1408119112743256134> thanks for sharing, I will put comments there.

author: maximmenshikov_
content:
<@1408119112743256134>  thank you, Marcin. We are pretty much aligned - you cover points that we work on. 

I need to recap, and need to stress more publicly, that we have a big test suite that has thousands of test cases for the compiler on different platforms, packages, modes, libc. We normally run smaller test suite subset for all commits, and much bigger test suite occasionally. What is important is that it catches real bugs on all levels with ease, usually, and we fixed many during development. .NET patches, compiler, jit fixes, whatever - all of that is caught.


There are a few points in which AI analysis is too paranoid. That's also good, let's harden that.

author: maximmenshikov_
content:
<@1408119112743256134> 
https://zk-testing.nethermind.dev/v2/log/760?mode=treeAndinfoAndlog&focusId=761
for example, one of logs. We run most tests in the console mode, though, and they are not reflected here.

author: lukaszrozmej
content: We will have a big upgrade excercise around November with dotnet 11. It will be nice reality check how much hussle it will be.

author: lukaszrozmej
content: <@1212698561221894166> we are very thankful for the feedback. We will go through points one-by-one. I instantly agree with some points on failing fast hardening.
